title: Imperial City of Alderaan
content:
{{Top|ncl}}
{{Otheruses|Imperial City}}
{{Noncanon|legends=1}}
[[File:RMQ-ImpCityofAlderaan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A [[Twin Ion Engine starfighter|T.I.E. fighter]] approaches the Imperial City.]]
The '''Imperial City of [[Alderaan (The Star Wars)|Alderaan]]''' was the capital of the [[New Galactic Empire]].

==Description==
It was a vast, platter shaped construct which floated amongst the cirrus methane clouds of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Alderaan (The Star Wars)|Alderaan]].

It was the capital of the [[Galactic Empire (The Star Wars)|New Galactic Empire]] and the home of the [[Emperor (The Star Wars)|Galactic Emperor]] and the [[Supreme Tribunal]] in the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]] of the script for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|A New Hope]] and the home of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Master of the Sith]] in the [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second draft]]. Security perimeters surrounded it into deep space.

[[File:ST_Lightsaber.png|thumb|left|180px|Inside the Imperial City]]

Triple guards, scramble scanners, gas locks and the isolation of each sector of the city helped to preserve its reputation of invulnerability.

After being captured above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]], [[Deak Starkiller]] was tortured and imprisoned in the Imperial City. He was released by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Montross Holdaack, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Starkiller]] and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|Artoo]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|Threepio]] after they sneaked inside by convincing the Imperial [[TIE/LN starfighter|TIE fighter]] patrols that they were adrift space junk.<ref name="AotS">''[[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars]]''</ref>

Places in the city included the [[Plaza of the Daders]] and a nightclub.<ref name="TSWRD">[[The Star Wars rough draft|''The Star Wars'' rough draft]]</ref>

The bowels of the Imperial City were infested with [[Dianoga/Legends|Dai Nogas]], deliberately introduced by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] as a security measure.<ref name="AotS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Imperial City of Alderaan is mentioned in the [[The Star Wars story synopsis|treatment]] for and appears in the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough]], [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second]], and [[The Star Wars: From The Adventures of Luke Starkiller|third]] drafts of the script for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]. The look of the Imperial City was designed by [[Ralph McQuarrie]] for the second draft. This look was re-used for [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. The Imperial City was deleted from the script for ''A New Hope'' when [[George Lucas]] wrote the fourth draft due to budget constraints and the scenes set in it were moved to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="Making of">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref>

Lucas, prior to the realization of Cloud City, described a similar environment for [[Corellia/Legends|"Crell"]] and mentioned [[Granicus]], the name Alderaan was known as in the [[The Star Wars first draft|first draft]] of the script for ''A New Hope'', in notes for licensees written in [[1977]].<ref name="Making of" />
